# Requirements: FlawChess — v1.25 Flaw-Stats Opponent Comparison (ARCHIVED)

**Status:** ✅ SHIPPED 2026-06-12 — all 18 active requirements Complete (1 voided: FLAWCMP-02; 1 voided pre-build: FLAWX-03).
**Defined:** 2026-06-09 · **Archived:** 2026-06-12 at milestone close.
**Core Value:** Position-precise WDL analysis (openings + endgames + time pressure) on top of users' actual chess.com / lichess games, with personalized LLM commentary and auto-generated strength/weakness reports. This milestone makes the Library flaw statistics *actionable* by contrasting the user against their actual opponents — flaw rates only reveal a *specific* recurring weakness when compared, not in absolute terms.

**Source:** `.planning/seeds/SEED-040-flaw-stats-opponent-comparison.md` (locked design decisions table). Continued phase numbering at **Phase 113**.

**Scope note (how this milestone evolved):** Two SEED-040 design premises were superseded during execution. FLAWX-03 (an `is_opponent` column + index) was voided before build in favour of a query-time `is_opponent_expr(ply, user_color)` helper (113-CONTEXT D-01/D-02/D-03). The count-rate-vs-proportion family split (FLAWCMP-01/02) was superseded by a unified per-100-moves paired-delta estimator for all 15 metrics (114-CONTEXT D-01/D-04), voiding FLAWCMP-02 (Wilson difference-of-proportions). The v2 requirement families FLAWTAC (SEED-039) and FLAWCOV (SEED-012) remain deferred and owned by their seeds.

## v1 Requirements

### Opponent-Flaw Materialization (FLAWX)

- [x] **FLAWX-01**: `game_flaws` records opponent flaws alongside player flaws.
  - **AMENDED (113-CONTEXT D-01):** the player/opponent split is **derived at query time** via a single `is_opponent_expr(ply, games.user_color)` repo helper, **not** stored as a column. Both sides' flaws persist as rows in existing columns at their own plies; the split is a read-time expression. — **Complete**
- [x] **FLAWX-02**: The player-only upsert filter is dropped so opponent flaws persist on **every** classify path (import hook, `reclassify_positions.py`, `backfill_flaws.py`), preserving the D-10 single-classify-path invariant; no engine cost added (both colors already evaluated). — **Complete**
- [~] **FLAWX-03**: ~~An Alembic migration adds `is_opponent` to `game_flaws` with index support.~~ — **VOIDED (113-CONTEXT D-02/D-03):** no column → no migration, no index. `is_opponent` is ~50% selective (no useful index) and a `GROUP BY` dimension, not a selective filter. The existing PK `(user_id, game_id, ply)` covers per-game two-sided reads; `(user_id, severity)` covers severity scans. **Replacement scope (D-04):** retrofit a player-only gate onto every existing `game_flaws` reader so opponent rows don't leak into the self-only UI — delivered under FLAWX-01.
- [x] **FLAWX-04**: `scripts/backfill_flaws.py` repopulates opponent flaws for existing analyzed games (dev users 28 & 44 and the benchmark cohort), idempotent and batched (OOM-safe); prod `game_flaws` continues to ship empty. — **Complete**

### Benchmark "Typical" Zone (FLAWBMK)

- [x] **FLAWBMK-01**: The benchmark pipeline computes, for each flaw-delta metric, every cohort user's own you−opponent delta over their own games (unified per-100-moves paired-delta for all metric families — see D-01 amendment), replicating the `flaws_service` classification over the cohort's moves. — **Complete**
- [x] **FLAWBMK-02**: The pipeline emits per-(ELO bucket × TC) **Q1/Q3 quartiles** of each delta plus ELO and TC marginals — not a full percentile CDF. — **Complete**
- [x] **FLAWBMK-03**: The established Cohen's-d collapse verdict runs per metric per axis ({ELO, TC}) to decide cell-specific vs single global zone. — **Complete**
- [x] **FLAWBMK-04**: The `/benchmarks` skill is extended to produce these flaw-delta quartiles, marginals, and collapse verdicts, written to the benchmark report under `reports/` (§5). — **Complete**

### You-vs-Opponent Comparison API (FLAWCMP)

- [x] **FLAWCMP-01**: The endpoint returns the per-metric you−opponent delta with a CI.
  - **AMENDED (114-CONTEXT D-01/D-04):** uses the **unified per-100-moves paired-delta for ALL 15 metrics** (no count-rate vs proportion split); CI via bootstrap/normal approx over per-game deltas, the game as pairing unit. — **Complete**
- [~] **FLAWCMP-02**: ~~For proportion families, return the you−opponent Wilson difference-of-proportions with its CI.~~ — **VOIDED (114-CONTEXT D-04):** superseded by the unified paired per-game delta estimator (D-01). Wilson difference-of-proportions not implemented.
- [x] **FLAWCMP-03**: The endpoint returns the full 15-bullet inventory (expanded to include `mistake`/`blunder` severity split) — per-tag delta + CI + (when available) benchmark zone bounds — honoring all existing game filters plus the severity filter. — **Complete**
- [x] **FLAWCMP-04**: The curated combo bullets `hasty + miss` (flagship) and `low-clock + miss` are included; their CI-width adequacy was validated against the materialized opponent-flaw data. — **Complete**
- [x] **FLAWCMP-05**: A section-level sample gate returns an "analyze more games" state below a floor N; above it every bullet returns measure + CI; a bullet returns a no-zone state only on literally zero events for that tag. — **Complete**

### Bullet-Grid Presentation (FLAWUI)

- [x] **FLAWUI-01**: The tag-distribution zone is replaced by a uniform grid of `MiniBulletChart` bullets, one per tag: measure (you−opponent delta), CI error bar, benchmark "typical" blue zone. — **Complete**
- [x] **FLAWUI-02**: Each bullet discloses metric definition, sign convention, and (for clock-conditioned tags) the tempo-interaction caveat, via the tooltip-popover pattern. — **Complete**
- [x] **FLAWUI-03**: A tooltip line discloses the filter×zone interaction (TC filter shifts the zone; user-local filters move only the point estimate). — **Complete**
- [x] **FLAWUI-04**: A bullet degrades gracefully — measure + CI render when events exist; the blue zone renders only when the cohort stat exists (future tactic-motif bullets render zoneless). — **Complete**
- [x] **FLAWUI-05**: The flaw trend chart remains comparison-free. — **Complete**
- [x] **FLAWUI-06**: The bullet grid works on mobile and follows `data-testid` / ARIA / semantic-HTML conventions, with desktop + mobile parity. — **Complete**

## v2 Requirements (Deferred — owned by their seeds)

### Tactic-Motif Comparison (FLAWTAC — SEED-039)

- **FLAWTAC-01**: Extend the comparison to tactic-motif families (missed forks/pins/skewers) once SEED-039 computes them; zoneless until cohort PVs exist.
- **FLAWTAC-02**: `miss` upgrades from the v1 proxy to literal `missed-X` via the SEED-039 adjacency join.
- **FLAWTAC-03**: Compute the benchmark blue zone for tactic-motif deltas (requires cohort-wide Stockfish PV lines).

### Eval-Coverage Enablement (FLAWCOV — SEED-012)

- **FLAWCOV-01**: Raise analyzed-game coverage (client-side `stockfish.wasm` and/or server idle-time analysis) so the comparison serves all users, not just the ~37–51 heavy-analysis users it ships for.

## Out of Scope

| Feature | Reason |
|---------|--------|
| Full benchmark **percentile CDF** on flaw deltas | Replaced by the light per-(ELO×TC) delta-IQR zone; the CDF cost trajectory does not survive the SEED-039 tactic-motif roadmap. |
| **Trend-chart** opponent comparison | ELO-peer matching already controls the absolute level; the signal is in the bullets' composition. |
| **Termination** patterns | Needs game-result/termination data, not `game_flaws`; belongs to the Time Management surface. |
| Direct **M-vs-B split** display | Single "Flaw Rate" bullet (M+B) for bigger N; the severity filter still narrows to blunders-only. |
| **Per-100 vs per-game denominator toggle** | Fixed at per-100 of your own moves (keeps the benchmark zone portable across ELO cells). |
| **Eval-coverage raising** | Owned by SEED-012 (v2/FLAWCOV); this milestone consumes existing coverage. |
